00489| :dp:`fls_q86j23iiqv8w`
00490| An :dt:`inclusive range pattern` is a :t:`range pattern` with both a
00491| :t:`range pattern low bound` and a :t:`range pattern high bound`.
00492| 
00493| :dp:`fls_3PyquOKjA7SI`
00494| An :dt:`exclusive range pattern` is a :t:`range pattern` with both a
00495| :t:`range pattern low bound` and a :t:`range pattern high bound`.
00496| 
00497| :dp:`fls_akf9x5r6e0ta`
00498| An :dt:`obsolete range pattern` is a :t:`range pattern` that uses obsolete syntax
00499| to express an :t:`inclusive range pattern`.
00500| 
00501| :dp:`fls_vrpr6ttpfpal`
00502| A :dt:`range pattern bound` is a constraint on the range of a :t:`range pattern`.
00503| 
00504| :dp:`fls_nk48gregn3me`
00505| A :dt:`range pattern low bound` is a :t:`range pattern bound` that specifies the
00506| start of a range.
00507| 
00508| :dp:`fls_83v1xqbebs58`
00509| A :dt:`range pattern high bound` is a :t:`range pattern bound` that specifies the
00510| end of a range.
00511| 
00512| :dp:`fls_2hpuccwh2xml`
00513| A :t:`half-open range pattern` shall appear within a :t:`parenthesized pattern`
00514| when the context is a :t:`slice pattern`.
00515| 
00516| :dp:`fls_9kk81isk0mlp`
00517| The :t:`range pattern low bound` of an :t:`inclusive range pattern` shall be
00518| less than or equal to its :t:`range pattern high bound`.
00519| 
00520| :dp:`fls_8bdOqkO1NuJW`
00521| The :t:`range pattern low bound` of an :t:`exclusive range pattern` shall be
00522| less than its :t:`range pattern high bound`.
00523| 
00524| :dp:`fls_s2b5n4snc4d7`
00525| An :t:`obsolete range pattern` is equivalent to an :t:`inclusive range pattern`.
00526| 
00527| :dp:`fls_4o4ge6x9a8rs`
00528| A :t:`range pattern` is an :t:`irrefutable pattern` only when it spans the
00529| entire set of possible :t:`[value]s` of a :t:`type`.
00530| 
00531| :dp:`fls_6o995ak4hywq`
00532| The :t:`[type]s` of the :t:`range pattern low bound` and the
00533| :t:`range pattern high bound` of a :t:`range pattern` shall be :t:`unifiable`.
00534| 
00535| :dp:`fls_3js1645tgh31`
00536| The :t:`type` of a :t:`range pattern` is determined as follows:
00537| 
00538| * :dp:`fls_8Q6NfRx4j5V7`
00539|   If the :t:`range pattern` is expressed as an :t:`inclusive range pattern`, an
00540|   :t:`exclusive range pattern`, or an :t:`obsolete range pattern`, then the
00541|   :t:`type` is the :t:`unified type` of the :t:`[type]s` of the
00542|   :t:`range pattern low bound` and the :t:`range pattern high bound`.
00543| 
00544| * :dp:`fls_rgr7t33s0m7m`
00545|   Otherwise the :t:`type` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`range pattern low bound`.
00546| 
00547| :dp:`fls_5ey5mj8t8knd`
00548| A :t:`path expression` of a :t:`range pattern` shall refer to a :t:`constant`
00549| of a :t:`scalar type`.
00550| 
00551| :dp:`fls_z4js96mchcsv`
00552| A :t:`qualified path expression` of a :t:`range pattern` shall refer to an
00553| :t:`associated constant` of a :t:`scalar type`.
00554| 
00555| .. rubric:: Examples
00556| 
00557| :dp:`fls_3wwpq8i6mo2a`
00558| Two range patterns in the context of a match expression.
00559| See :p:`fls_sfyfdxhvhk44` for the declaration of ``x``.
00560| 
00561| .. code-block:: rust
00562| 
00563|    match x {
00564|        -30 ..= 2 => (),
00565|        57 .. => (),
00566|        _ => (),
00567|    }
00568| 
00569| .. _fls_d2sc9hl3v0mk:
00570| 
00571| Reference Patterns
00572| ------------------
00573| 
00574| .. rubric:: Syntax
00575| 
00576| .. syntax::
00577| 
00578|    ReferencePattern ::=
00579|        $$&$$ $$mut$$? PatternWithoutRange
00580| 
00581| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00582| 
00583| :dp:`fls_fhahcc1mz2qh`
00584| A :dt:`reference pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that dereferences a :t:`pointer` that
00585| is being matched.
00586| 
00587| :dp:`fls_x0bmzl1315gq`
00588| A :t:`reference pattern` is an :t:`irrefutable pattern` when its nested :t:`pattern` itself is an :t:`irrefutable pattern`.
00589| 
00590| :dp:`fls_fedo8zhgpla5`
00591| The :t:`type` of a :t:`reference pattern` is determined as follows:
00592| 
00593| * :dp:`fls_30u9ij164ww3`
00594|   If the :t:`reference pattern` appears with :t:`keyword` ``mut``,
00595|   then the :t:`type` is ``&mut pattern_without_range_type``,
00596|   where ``pattern_without_range_type`` is the :t:`type` of the
00597|   :s:`PatternWithoutRange`.
00598| 
00599| * :dp:`fls_d1kc73hpncpo`
00600|   If the :t:`reference pattern` appears without :t:`keyword`
00601|   ``mut``, then the :t:`type` is ``& pattern_without_range_type``,
00602|   where ``pattern_without_range_type`` is the :t:`type` of the
00603|   :s:`PatternWithoutRange`.
00604| 
00605| .. rubric:: Examples
00606| 
00607| :dp:`fls_mpeuhov0umfa`
00608| A reference pattern in the context of a match expression.
00609| See :p:`fls_2xq8852gihn9` for the declaration of ``ref_x``.
00610| 
00611| .. code-block:: rust
00612| 
00613|    match ref_x {
00614|        &23 => (),
00615|        _ => (),
00616|    }
00617| 
00618| .. _fls_7wpgnp4kjq82:
00619| 
00620| Rest Patterns
00621| -------------
00622| 
00623| .. rubric:: Syntax
00624| 
00625| .. syntax::
00626| 
00627|    RestPattern ::=
00628|        $$..$$
00629| 
00630| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00631| 
00632| :dp:`fls_eso51epfofxb`
00633| A :dt:`rest pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that matches zero or more elements that
00634| have not already been matched.
00635| 
00636| :dp:`fls_5a75a2y43uev`
00637| A :t:`rest pattern` shall appear at most once within a :t:`slice pattern`, an
00638| :t:`identifier pattern` of a :t:`slice pattern`, a :t:`tuple pattern`, and a
00639| :t:`tuple struct pattern`.
00640| 
00641| :dp:`fls_rsqyza99vl3x`
00642| A :t:`rest pattern` is an :t:`irrefutable pattern`.
00643| 
00644| :dp:`fls_w1pw40phsv2o`
00645| If a :t:`rest pattern` appears within a :t:`slice pattern` or the
00646| :t:`identifier pattern` of a :t:`slice pattern`, then the :t:`type` of the
00647| :t:`rest pattern` is determined as follows:
00648| 
00649| * :dp:`fls_x8ylgxrf9ca`
00650|   If the :t:`type` of the :t:`slice pattern` is an :t:`array type`, then the
00651|   :t:`type` is ``[T; N]`` where ``T`` is the :t:`element type` of the
00652|   :t:`array type`, and ``N`` is the :t:`[array type]'s` size minus the number
00653|   of matched elements of the :t:`slice pattern`.
00654| 
00655| * :dp:`fls_zgoke73xrhk3`
00656|   If the :t:`type` of the :t:`slice pattern` is a :t:`slice type`, then the
00657|   :t:`type` is that :t:`slice type`.
00658| 
00659| .. rubric:: Examples
00660| 
00661| :dp:`fls_bdcv6rwx0fsv`
00662| A rest pattern in an identifier pattern of a slice pattern, followed by a rest
00663| pattern in a slice pattern.
00664| 
00665| .. code-block:: rust
00666| 
00667|    match slice {
00668|        [1, 5, .., 7] => (),
00669|        [start, end @ ..] => (),
00670|    }
00671| 
00672| :dp:`fls_qz9guhlg19j3`
00673| Rest patterns in tuple patterns.
00674| 
00675| .. syntax::
00676| 
00677|    match tuple {
00678|        (1, .., y) => (),
00679|        (.., 5) => (),
00680|        (..) => (),
00681|    }
00682| 
00683| .. _fls_qte70mgzpras:
00684| 
00685| Slice Patterns
00686| --------------
00687| 
00688| .. rubric:: Syntax
00689| 
00690| .. syntax::
00691| 
00692|    SlicePattern ::=
00693|        $$[$$ PatternList? $$]$$
00694| 
00695| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00696| 
00697| :dp:`fls_qqiu594hki8g`
00698| A :dt:`slice pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that matches :t:`[array]s` of fixed size
00699| and :t:`[slice]s` of dynamic size.
00700| 
00701| :dp:`fls_h6x9xlxi7y5n`
00702| A :t:`slice pattern` is an :t:`irrefutable pattern` when it refers to:
00703| 
00704| * :dp:`fls_jbmxu7y5fnm6`
00705|   An :t:`array`, where each :t:`subpattern` is an :t:`irrefutable pattern`, or
00706| 
00707| * :dp:`fls_r78zzw7yyg34`
00708|   A :t:`slice`, where the :s:`PatternList` consists of a single
00709|   :t:`rest pattern`, or a single possibly nested :t:`identifier pattern` whose
00710|   last :t:`bound pattern` is a :t:`rest pattern`.
00711| 
00712| :dp:`fls_ndor56nou676`
00713| The :t:`type` of a :t:`slice pattern` is the same as the :t:`expected type`.
00714| 
00715| .. rubric:: Examples
00716| 
00717| .. syntax::
00718| 
00719|    let v = vec![1, 2, 3];
00720| 
00721| :dp:`fls_9yuobz1jsehf`
00722| A slice pattern in the context of a match expression.
00723| 
00724| .. syntax::
00725| 
00726|    match v.as_slice() {
00727|        [a, b, c] => (),
00728|        _ => ()
00729|    }
00730| 
00731| .. _fls_7dbd5t2750ce:
00732| 
00733| Struct Patterns
00734| ---------------
00735| 
00736| .. rubric:: Syntax
00737| 
00738| .. syntax::
00739| 
00740|    StructPattern ::=
00741|        RecordStructPattern
00742|      | TupleStructPattern
00743| 
00744|    Deconstructee ::=
00745|        PathExpression
00746| 
00747| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00748| 
00749| :dp:`fls_vjdkpr3zml51`
00750| A :dt:`struct pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that matches an :t:`enum value`, a
00751| :t:`struct value`, or a :t:`union value`.
00752| 
00753| :dp:`fls_6o3x101wo478`
00754| A :dt:`deconstructee` indicates the :t:`enum variant` or :t:`type` that is being
00755| deconstructed by a :t:`struct pattern`.
00756| 
00757| :dp:`fls_k9zih9s0oe5h`
00758| A :t:`struct pattern` is interpreted based on the :t:`deconstructee`. It is a
00759| static error if a :t:`struct pattern` cannot be interpreted.
00760| 
00761| :dp:`fls_r8rat3qmc4hy`
00762| A :t:`struct pattern` is an :t:`irrefutable pattern` if
00763| 
00764| * :dp:`fls_hUX723DmLg2a`
00765|   All of its :t:`[subpattern]s` are :t:`[irrefutable pattern]s`, and
00766| 
00767| * :dp:`fls_p4OplpUvS04l`
00768|   The :t:`deconstructee` is either:
00769| 
00770|   * :dp:`fls_pre3YwAv01FE`
00771|     A :t:`struct type` or a :t:`union type`, or
00772| 
00773|   * :dp:`fls_MK83WE0iDqNf`
00774|     An :t:`enum variant` of an :t:`enum type` with zero or more :t:`[visible empty enum variant]s` and one non-:t:`visible empty enum variant` where the :t:`[type]s` of all :t:`[field]s` are not subject to :t:`visible emptiness`.
00775| 
00776| .. _fls_nruvg0es3kx7:
00777| 
00778| Record Struct Patterns
00779| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00780| 
00781| .. rubric:: Syntax
00782| 
00783| .. syntax::
00784| 
00785|    RecordStructPattern ::=
00786|        Deconstructee $${$$ RecordStructPatternContent? $$}$$
00787| 
00788|    RecordStructPatternContent ::=
00789|        RecordStructRestPattern
00790|      | FieldDeconstructorList ($$,$$ RecordStructRestPattern | $$,$$?)
00791| 
00792|    RecordStructRestPattern ::=
00793|        OuterAttributeOrDoc* RestPattern
00794| 
00795|    FieldDeconstructorList ::=
00796|        FieldDeconstructor ($$,$$ FieldDeconstructor)*
00797| 
00798|    FieldDeconstructor ::=
00799|        OuterAttributeOrDoc* (
00800|            IndexedDeconstructor
00801|          | NamedDeconstructor
00802|          | ShorthandDeconstructor
00803|        )
00804| 
00805|    IndexedDeconstructor ::=
00806|        FieldIndex $$:$$ Pattern
00807| 
00808|    NamedDeconstructor ::=
00809|        Identifier $$:$$ Pattern
00810| 
00811|    ShorthandDeconstructor ::=
00812|        $$ref$$? $$mut$$? Binding
00813| 
00814|    FieldIndex ::=
00815|        DecimalLiteral
00816| 
00817| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00818| 
00819| :dp:`fls_g6dytd6aq62d`
00820| A :dt:`record struct pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that matches a
00821| :t:`enum variant value`, a :t:`struct value`, or a :t:`union value`.
00822| 
00823| :dp:`fls_3px4oiweg9dm`
00824| The :t:`deconstructee` of a :t:`record struct pattern` shall resolve to an
00825| :t:`enum variant`, a :t:`struct type`, or a :t:`union type`.
00826| 
00827| :dp:`fls_mnh35ehva8tx`
00828| An :dt:`indexed deconstructor` is a :t:`construct` that matches the position of
00829| a :t:`field`.
00830| 
00831| :dp:`fls_p2rjnlbvifaa`
00832| An :t:`indexed deconstructor` matches a :t:`field` of the :t:`deconstructee`
00833| when its :t:`field index` and the position of the :t:`field` in the
00834| :t:`deconstructee` are the same. Such an :t:`indexed deconstructor` is a
00835| :dt:`matched indexed deconstructor`.
00836| 
00837| :dp:`fls_23be2x50at14`
00838| The :t:`type` of a :t:`matched indexed deconstructor` and the :t:`type` of the
00839| matched :t:`field` shall be :t:`unifiable`.
00840| 
00841| :dp:`fls_46u4ddj0yf93`
00842| A :dt:`named deconstructor` is a :t:`construct` that matches the :t:`name` of
00843| a :t:`field`.
00844| 
00845| :dp:`fls_qu3dvfdq6oy7`
00846| A :t:`named deconstructor` matches a :t:`field` of the :t:`deconstructee` when
00847| its :t:`identifier` and the :t:`name` of the :t:`field` are the same. Such a
00848| :t:`named deconstructor` is a :dt:`matched named deconstructor`.
00849| 
00850| :dp:`fls_4b2hchdzv30u`
00851| The :t:`type` of a :t:`matched named deconstructor` and the :t:`type` of the
00852| matched :t:`field` shall be :t:`unifiable`.
00853| 
00854| :dp:`fls_9wfizujx0szd`
00855| A :dt:`shorthand deconstructor` is a :t:`construct` that matches the :t:`name`
00856| of a :t:`field` and binds the :t:`value` of the matched :t:`field` to a
00857| :t:`binding`.
00858| 
00859| :dp:`fls_jTh9Hur0qsIb`
00860| A :t:`shorthand deconstructor` with :t:`keyword` ``mut`` yields a
00861| :t:`mutable binding`.
00862| 
00863| :dp:`fls_as54u97xis8z`
00864| It is a static error if a :t:`shorthand deconstructor` has only :t:`keyword`
00865| ``ref`` or :t:`[keyword]s` ``ref`` ``mut``, and its :t:`binding` shadows a
00866| :t:`constant`, a :t:`unit enum variant`, or a :t:`unit struct constant`.
00867| 
00868| :dp:`fls_8364ueejn5y3`
00869| A :t:`shorthand deconstructor` is equivalent to a :t:`named deconstructor` where
00870| the :t:`name` of the :t:`shorthand deconstructor` denotes the :t:`identifier`
00871| of the :t:`named deconstructor` and the entire content of the :t:`shorthand
00872| deconstructor` denotes the :t:`pattern` of the :t:`named deconstructor`.
00873| 
00874| :dp:`fls_7t0be1w2hq3c`
00875| A :t:`shorthand deconstructor` matches a :t:`field` of the :t:`deconstructee`
00876| when its :t:`name` and the :t:`name` of the :t:`field` are the same. Such a
00877| :t:`shorthand deconstructor` is a :dt:`matched shorthand deconstructor`.
00878| 
00879| :dp:`fls_3vgmkm2mzwwy`
00880| The :t:`type` of a :t:`matched shorthand deconstructor` and the :t:`type` of the
00881| matched :t:`field` shall be :t:`unifiable`.
00882| 
00883| :dp:`fls_m91ith3rjy79`
00884| If the :t:`deconstructee` of a :t:`record struct pattern` is a
00885| :t:`record enum variant` or a :t:`record struct`, then
00886| 
00887| * :dp:`fls_c09jf2vpcr58`
00888|   For each :t:`field` of the :t:`deconstructee`, the :t:`record struct pattern`
00889|   shall either:
00890| 
00891|   * :dp:`fls_4h00oqypa8qg`
00892|     Contain at most one :t:`matched named deconstructor`, or
00893| 
00894|   * :dp:`fls_195mqijyrnam`
00895|     Contain at most one :t:`matched shorthand deconstructor`, or
00896| 
00897|   * :dp:`fls_ta0vdoqmt2k1`
00898|     Have exactly one :s:`RecordStructRestPattern`.
00899| 
00900| * :dp:`fls_f0u0j4q90lpl`
00901|   A :s:`RecordStructRestPattern` is allowed even if all :t:`[field]s` of the
00902|   :t:`deconstructee` have been matched.
00903| 
00904| :dp:`fls_8bi8q3usubby`
00905| If the :t:`deconstructee` of a :t:`record struct pattern` is a
00906| :t:`tuple enum variant` or a :t:`tuple struct type`, then
00907| 
00908| * :dp:`fls_1x0o71kxj3yq`
00909|   For each :t:`field` of the :t:`deconstructee`, the :t:`record struct pattern`
00910|   shall either:
00911| 
00912|   * :dp:`fls_1thgpx95lfg5`
00913|     Contain at most one :t:`matched indexed deconstructor`, or
00914| 
00915|   * :dp:`fls_rpo1wimbmzhc`
00916|     Have exactly one ``RecordStructRestPattern.``
00917| 
00918| * :dp:`fls_brhtaaxt1s3s`
00919|   A :s:`RecordStructRestPattern` is allowed even if all :t:`[field]s` of the
00920|   :t:`deconstructee` have been matched.
00921| 
00922| :dp:`fls_jwz3arnfkxwn`
00923| If the :t:`deconstructee` of a :t:`record struct pattern` is a :t:`union type`,
00924| then
00925| 
00926| * :dp:`fls_pfz8xlwezbw1`
00927|   The :s:`RecordStructPatternContent` of the :t:`record struct
00928|   pattern` shall contain exactly one :s:`FieldDeconstructor`.
00929| 
00930| * :dp:`fls_XFKBJZe6k1o2`
00931|   The :t:`record struct pattern` shall not contain a
00932|   :s:`RecordStructRestPattern`.
00933| 
00934| * :dp:`fls_mu166csowj71`
00935|   For the single :t:`field` of the :t:`deconstructee`, the
00936|   :t:`record struct pattern` shall either:
00937| 
00938|   * :dp:`fls_y09fygnglu3n`
00939|     Contain exactly one :t:`matched named deconstructor`, or
00940| 
00941|   * :dp:`fls_2tadaatmauzk`
00942|     Contain exactly one :t:`matched shorthand deconstructor`.
00943| 
00944| * :dp:`fls_oq30xkmvyz72`
00945|   The :t:`record struct pattern` shall require :t:`unsafe context`.
00946| 
00947| :dp:`fls_9y1gbv47z23o`
00948| If the :t:`deconstructee` of a :t:`record struct pattern` is a
00949| :t:`unit enum variant` or a :t:`unit struct`, then the
00950| :t:`record struct pattern` shall have at most one :s:`RecordStructRestPattern`.
00951| 
00952| .. rubric:: Undefined Behavior
00953| 
00954| :dp:`fls_dHtV2BPRFVBB`
00955| It is undefined behavior reading the :t:`field` of a :t:`deconstructee` that is
00956| a :t:`union type` when the :t:`field` contains data that is invalid for the
00957| :t:`field`'s type.
00958| 
00959| .. rubric:: Examples
00960| 
00961| :dp:`fls_zRCiKnhQebyp`
00962| A record struct pattern deconstructing a record struct.
00963| 
00964| .. code-block:: rust
00965| 
00966|    struct RecordStruct {
00967|        first : u32,
00968|        second: u32,
00969|    }
00970| 
00971|    let record_struct_value = RecordStruct { first: 11, second: 22 };
00972| 
00973|    match record_struct_value {
00974|        RecordStruct { second: 33, ref first } => (),
00975|        RecordStruct { first: 44, .. } => (),
00976|        RecordStruct { .. } => (),
00977|    }
00978| 
00979| :dp:`fls_D5tAGzrjXFTu`
00980| A record struct pattern deconstructing a tuple struct.
00981| 
00982| .. code-block:: rust
00983| 
00984|    struct TupleStruct (
00985|        u32,
00986|        u32,
00987|    );
00988| 
00989|    let tuple_struct_value = TupleStruct { 0: 11, 1: 22 };
00990| 
00991|    match tuple_struct_value {
00992|        TupleStruct { 1: 33, 0: 44 } => (),
00993|        TupleStruct { 0: 55, .. } => (),
00994|        TupleStruct { .. } => (),
00995|    }
00996| 
00997| :dp:`fls_FhvMzLPRlY7p`
00998| A record struct pattern deconstructing a union.
00999| 
01000| .. code-block:: rust
01001| 
01002|    union Union {
01003|        first : u32,
01004|        second: u32,
01005|    }
01006| 
01007|    let union_value = Union { second: 11 };
01008| 
01009|    unsafe {
01010|        match union_value {
01011|            Union { first: 22 } => (),
01012|            Union { second: 33 } => (),
01013|            _ => (),
01014|        }
01015|    }
01016| 
01017| .. _fls_vlrto778v49m:
01018| 
01019| Tuple Struct Patterns
01020| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01021| 
01022| .. rubric:: Syntax
01023| 
01024| .. syntax::
01025| 
01026|    TupleStructPattern ::=
01027|        Deconstructee $$($$ PatternList? $$)$$
01028| 
01029| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01030| 
01031| :dp:`fls_ks6y1syab2bp`
01032| A :dt:`tuple struct pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that matches a
01033| :t:`tuple enum variant value` or a :t:`tuple struct value`.
01034| 
01035| :dp:`fls_t1mrijw16k9a`
01036| The :t:`deconstructee` of a :t:`tuple struct pattern` shall resolve to a
01037| :t:`tuple enum variant` or a :t:`tuple struct type`.
01038| 
01039| :dp:`fls_ryfcrqrkp28y`
01040| A :t:`subpattern` of a :t:`tuple struct pattern` matches a :t:`field` of the
01041| :t:`deconstructee` when its position and the position of the :t:`field` in
01042| the :t:`deconstructee` are the same. Such a :t:`subpattern` is a
01043| :dt:`matched tuple struct subpattern`.
01044| 
01045| :dp:`fls_ehf9r6halgh1`
01046| The position of a :t:`subpattern` is determined as follows:
01047| 
01048| * :dp:`fls_5lo1hs8wzz0t`

01053|     position is the position within the :s:`PatternList` in left-to-right order,

01056|   * :dp:`fls_w369n8lmwr7g`

01061| * :dp:`fls_4is6h95jj3gd`

01066| The :t:`type` of the :t:`subpattern` of a :t:`tuple struct pattern` and the
01067| :t:`type` of the matched :t:`field` shall be :t:`unifiable`.

A :dt:`pattern` is a :t:`construct` that matches a :t:`value` which satisfies all
the criteria of the :t:`pattern`.

:dp:`fls_VQMmveZUfNTn`
An :dt:`or-pattern` is a :t:`pattern` that matches on one of two or more :t:`[pattern-without-alternation]s` and or-s them using character 0x7C (vertical line).

:dp:`fls_mp6i4blzexnu`
A :dt:`pattern-without-alternation` is a :t:`pattern` that cannot be alternated.

:dp:`fls_JJ1fJa1SsaWh`
A :dt:`pattern-without-range` is a :t:`pattern-without-alternation` that
excludes :t:`[range pattern]s`.

:dp:`fls_6xx34zr069bj`
A :dt:`subpattern` is a :t:`pattern` nested within another pattern.

:dp:`fls_8xzjb0yzftkd`
A :t:`pattern` has a :t:`type`, with the exception of the :t:`rest pattern` if
it is not the inner :t:`pattern` of a :t:`slice pattern` or the :t:`pattern` of
a possibly nested :t:`identifier pattern` of a :t:`slice pattern`\ ``.``

00332| 
00333| :dp:`fls_od0h3v40kjp6`
00334| An invocation of the :std:`core::ptr::addr_of` :t:`macro` expands to a
00335| :t:`constant expression` allowed in any :t:`constant context` and
00336| :t:`constant function`, subject to the same restrictions as a

00338| 
00339| :dp:`fls_6sc556tz4oxd`
00340| An invocation of the :std:`core::panic` :t:`macro` expands to a
00341| :t:`constant expression` allowed in any :t:`constant context` and
00342| :t:`constant function`, as long as the :t:`macro` is either invoked without
00343| arguments, or with a single :t:`string literal` that does not

00345| 
00346| :dp:`fls_b1vfpvsdv38`
00347| A :t:`constant expression` is evaluated statically whenever its :t:`value` is

00350| :dp:`fls_b46nyamfqxdu`
00351| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`constant expression` that results in

00364| :dp:`fls_tg0kya5125jt`

00393|   :t:`[Call expression]s` and :t:`[method call expression]s` if the :t:`return
00394|   type` of the :t:`call operand` is the :t:`never type`,

00397|   Invocations of the :std:`core::panic` :t:`macro`,

00405| Place Expressions
00406| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00407| 
00408| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00409| 
00410| :dp:`fls_yBEzAdfSAMYQ`
00411| A :dt:`place` is a location where a :t:`value` resides.
00412| 
00413| :dp:`fls_qbrcg3cl9td`
00414| A :dt:`place expression` is an :t:`expression` that represents a memory
00415| location. The following :t:`[expression]s` are :t:`[place expression]s`:
00416| 
00417| * :dp:`fls_jpmhibm4omm7`
00418|   :t:`[Dereference expression]s`,
00419| 
00420| * :dp:`fls_none1dykbn8c`
00421|   :t:`[Field access expression]s`,
00422| 
00423| * :dp:`fls_lj7x5dgbmg9i`
00424|   :t:`[Index expression]s`,
00425| 
00426| * :dp:`fls_anzidgx02lly`
00427|   :t:`[Parenthesized expression]s` where the :t:`operand` is a
00428|   :t:`place expression`,
00429| 
00430| * :dp:`fls_ya05djl1d154`
00431|   :t:`[Path expression]s` that resolve to a :t:`static` or a :t:`variable`.
00432| 
00433| * :dp:`fls_gv4M0DE3OMwk`
00434|   A :t:`temporary`.
00435| 
00436| :dp:`fls_ku38h562vfyl`
00437| A :dt:`mutable place expression` is a :t:`place expression` whose memory
00438| location can be modified. The following :t:`[place expression]s` are
00439| :t:`[mutable place expression]s`:
00440| 
00441| * :dp:`fls_1tq2o2huda9l`
00442|   A :t:`dereference expression` whose :t:`operand`'s :t:`type` implements the
00443|   :std:`core::ops::DerefMut` :t:`trait`,
00444| 
00445| * :dp:`fls_6b4rwkrc1ap6`
00446|   A :t:`dereference expression` whose :t:`operand`'s :t:`type` is a
00447|   :t:`mutable raw pointer type`,
00448| 
00449| * :dp:`fls_s4bhrpykzmm7`
00450|   A :t:`dereference expression` whose :t:`operand`'s :t:`type` is a
00451|   :t:`mutable reference type`,
00452| 
00453| * :dp:`fls_xm0gm2q27x2e`
00454|   A :t:`field access expression` where the :t:`container operand` is a
00455|   :t:`mutable place expression`,
00456| 
00457| * :dp:`fls_bt50fltfqcvn`
00458|   An :t:`index expression` whose :t:`type` implements the
00459|   :std:`core::ops::IndexMut` :t:`trait`,
00460| 
00461| * :dp:`fls_Sgfxp186GMvz`
00462|   :t:`[Parenthesized expression]s` where the :t:`operand` is a
00463|   :t:`mutable place expression`,
00464| 
00465| * :dp:`fls_ilaqmj3hc5uv`
00466|   A :t:`path expression` that resolves to a :t:`mutable static`,
00467| 
00468| * :dp:`fls_m0gbw9myylv2`
00469|   A :t:`path expression` that resolves to a :t:`mutable variable` that is not
00470|   currently borrowed,
00471| 
00472| * :dp:`fls_dcm3yr3y9y0a`
00473|   A :t:`temporary`.
00474| 
00475| :dp:`fls_cPEMHZtPkctX`
00476| An :dt:`immutable place expression` is a :t:`place expression` whose memory
00477| location cannot be modified. All :t:`[place expression]s` that are not
00478| :t:`[mutable place expression]s` are :t:`[immutable place expression]s`.
00479| 
00480| :dp:`fls_4vxi1ji93dxb`
00481| A :dt:`place expression context` is a :t:`construct` that may evaluate its
00482| :t:`operand` as a memory location.
00483| 
00484| :dp:`fls_fzsrdrHnndRd`
00485| The following :t:`[construct]s` are :t:`[place expression context]s`:
00486| 
00487| * :dp:`fls_ZED5wJQVO6nf`
00488|   The :t:`assignee operand` of an :t:`assignment expression` or a
00489|   :t:`compound assignment expression`,
00490| 
00491| * :dp:`fls_Ufz9W5vyZkv3`
00492|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`borrow expression`,
00493| 
00494| * :dp:`fls_K7SbApHPmwjM`
00495|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`raw borrow expression`,
00496| 
00497| * :dp:`fls_KxWIzoh9WwK7`
00498|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression`,
00499| 
00500| * :dp:`fls_oV9Hd6OiFAZX`
00501|   The :t:`container operand` of a :t:`field access expression`,
00502| 
00503| * :dp:`fls_NnGiNsH6Zgmp`
00504|   The initialization :t:`expression` of a :t:`let statement`,
00505| 
00506| * :dp:`fls_jLZlxIHr4w2v`
00507|   The :t:`operand` of an :t:`implicit borrow`,
00508| 
00509| * :dp:`fls_giZ7w1G02JSg`
00510|   The :t:`indexed operand` of an :t:`index expression`,
00511| 
00512| * :dp:`fls_5yXuTLQOQ3cc`
00513|   The :t:`subject let expression` of an :t:`if let expression` or a
00514|   :t:`while let loop expression`,
00515| 
00516| * :dp:`fls_nman7mJVSQlm`
00517|   The :t:`subject expression` of a :t:`match expression`,
00518| 
00519| * :dp:`fls_JBfZuFDQg3mU`
00520|   The :t:`base initializer` of a :t:`struct expression`.
00521| 
00522| :dp:`fls_wxGAOWEVT77u`
00523| A :dt:`mutable place expression context` is a :t:`place expression context` that
00524| may evaluate its :t:`operand` as a mutable memory location. The following
00525| :t:`[construct]s` are :t:`[mutable place expression context]s`:
00526|

00530| 
00531| * :dp:`fls_5gy92rsi2mqm`
00532|   The :t:`assignee operand` of an :t:`assignment expression` or a

00534| 
00535| * :dp:`fls_u80htrnr2ebz`
00536|   The :t:`operand` of a mutable :t:`borrow expression`,
00537| 
00538| * :dp:`fls_o0feajus3jtu`
00539|   The :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression` if evaluated in a

00541| 
00542| * :dp:`fls_ffjx1d5dseo4`
00543|   The :t:`container operand` of :t:`field access expression` if evaluated in a
00544|   :t:`mutable place expression context`,
00545| 
00546| * :dp:`fls_9r7dopqf1nzl`
00547|   The :t:`subject let expression` of an :t:`if let expression` or a
00548|   :t:`while let loop expression`,
00549| 
00550| * :dp:`fls_o76QXHyrrJPG`
00551|   The :t:`operand` of a mutable :t:`implicit borrow`,
00552| 
00553| * :dp:`fls_ka5b87tkf8t6`
00554|   The initialization :t:`expression` of a :t:`let statement`,
00555| 
00556| * :dp:`fls_brwv1zwu37e8`
00557|   The :t:`subject expression` of a :t:`match expression`,
00558| 
00559| :dp:`fls_YHj3sB09Wbyb`
00560| An :dt:`immutable place expression context` is a :t:`place expression context`
00561| that may evaluate its :t:`operand` as an immutable memory location. All
00562| :t:`[place expression context]s` that are not
00563| :t:`[mutable place expression context]s` are
00564| :t:`[immutable place expression context]s`.
00565| 
00566| :dp:`fls_4axr4V0icdBP`
00567| A :t:`place expression` that is evaluated in a :t:`value expression context`
00568| or bound :t:`by value` in a :t:`pattern` denotes the :t:`value` held in the

00571| 
00572| .. _fls_e7zgqroy2qxn:
00573| 
00574| Value Expressions
00575| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00576| 
00577| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00578| 
00579| :dp:`fls_7q4hrt6yfr9b`
00580| A :dt:`value expression` is an :t:`expression` that represents a :t:`value`.
00581| All :t:`[expression]s` that are not :t:`[place expression]s` are
00582| :t:`[value expression]s`.
00583| 
00584| :dp:`fls_pB6xlp4uAg37`
00585| A :dt:`value expression context` is an expression context that is not a
00586| :t:`place expression context`.
00587| 
00588| :dp:`fls_8uhfwqurbyqf`
00589| The evaluation of a :t:`value expression` in a :t:`place expression context`
00590| shall evaluate the :t:`value expression` as a :t:`temporary` and then use the
00591| :t:`temporary` in the :t:`place expression context`.
00592| 
00593| .. _fls_h0dvogc64tfh:
00594| 
00595| Literal Expressions
00596| -------------------
00597| 
00598| .. rubric:: Syntax
00599| 
00600| .. syntax::
00601| 
00602|    LiteralExpression ::=
00603|        Literal
00604| 
00605| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00606| 
00607| :dp:`fls_rbwwczom3agt`
00608| A :dt:`literal expression` is an :t:`expression` that denotes a :t:`literal`.
00609| 
00610| :dp:`fls_w30su9x4q13r`
00611| The :t:`type` of a :t:`literal expression` is the :t:`type` of the corresponding
00612| :t:`literal`.
00613| 
00614| :dp:`fls_wdpbg5xzgmwu`
00615| The :t:`value` of a :t:`literal expression` is the :t:`value` of the
00616| corresponding :t:`literal`.
00617| 
00618| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00619| 
00620| :dp:`fls_g061yzws1m45`
00621| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`literal expression` has no effect.
00622| 
00623| .. rubric:: Examples
00624| 
00625| .. code-block:: rust
00626| 
00627|    5
00628|    'a'
00629|    "hello"

00633| Path Expressions
00634| ----------------
00635| 
00636| .. rubric:: Syntax
00637| 
00638| .. syntax::
00639| 
00640|    PathExpression ::=
00641|        UnqualifiedPathExpression
00642|      | QualifiedPathExpression
00643| 
00644| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00645| 
00646| :dp:`fls_gvanx4874ycy`
00647| A :dt:`path expression` is an :t:`expression` that denotes a :t:`path`.
00648| 
00649| :dp:`fls_EOkrcIj9CuhV`
00650| A :t:`path expression` shall resolve to either a :t:`constant parameter`, a
00651| :t:`constant`, a :t:`function`, a :t:`static`, a :t:`tuple enum variant`, a
00652| :t:`tuple struct`, a :t:`unit enum variant`, a :t:`unit struct`, or a
00653| :t:`variable`.
00654| 
00655| :dp:`fls_gz67ju6l7uhn`
00656| A :t:`path expression` that resolves to a :t:`mutable static` shall require
00657| :t:`unsafe context`.
00658| 
00659| :dp:`fls_cjywisyiyti6`
00660| The :t:`type` of a :t:`path expression` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`entity` that
00661| it resolved to.
00662| 
00663| :dp:`fls_5ifai8nkp5ek`
00664| The :t:`value` of a :t:`path expression` is the :t:`entity` that it resolved to.
00665| 
00666| .. rubric:: Examples
00667| 
00668| .. code-block:: rust
00669| 
00670|    globals::STATIC_VARIABLE

00680| .. syntax::
00681| 
00682|    BlockExpression ::=
00683|        $${$$
00684|          InnerAttributeOrDoc*
00685|          StatementList
00686|        $$}$$
00687| 
00688|    StatementList ::=
00689|        Statement* Expression?
00690| 
00691| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00692| 
00693| :dp:`fls_nf65p0l0v0gr`
00694| A :dt:`block expression` is an :t:`expression` that sequences :t:`[expression]s`
00695| and :t:`[statement]s`.
00696| 
00697| :dp:`fls_tn3hj7k2lliu`
00698| A :dt:`tail expression` is the last :t:`expression` within a :t:`block
00699| expression`.
00700| 
00701| :dp:`fls_DfCne8YWevLE`
00702| When the remaining :t:`[lexical element]s` of a :s:`StatementList` match either
00703| an :s:`Expression` or :s:`Statement` they are interpreted as an :s:`Expression`.
00704| 
00705| :dp:`fls_u4gj2lnkq9ub`
00706| The :t:`type` of a :t:`block expression` is determined as follows:
00707| 
00708| * :dp:`fls_ltEygvWDtHXE`
00709|   If the :t:`block expression` contains at least one :t:`break expression` and
00710|   has a :t:`tail expression`, then the :t:`type` is the :t:`unified type` of
00711|   the :t:`[break type]s` of all :t:`[break expression]s` and the :t:`type` of the
00712|   :t:`tail expression`.
00713| 
00714| * :dp:`fls_97v4fnekrRXI`
00715|   Otherwise, if the :t:`block expression` contains at least one
00716|   :t:`break expression`, then the :t:`type` is the :t:`unified type` of the
00717|   :t:`[break type]s` of all :t:`[break expression]s`.
00718| 
00719| * :dp:`fls_ob76y2ymdd27`
00720|   Otherwise, if the :t:`block expression` has a :t:`tail expression`, then the
00721|   :t:`type` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`tail expression`.
00722| 
00723| * :dp:`fls_u0avbm147nyh`
00724|   Otherwise the :t:`type` is the :t:`unit type`.
00725| 
00726| :dp:`fls_1hzup0sf8l7l`
00727| The :t:`value` of a :t:`block expression` is determined as follows:
00728| 
00729| * :dp:`fls_kKZPKvJ902cw`
00730|   If the :t:`block expression` contains at least one :t:`break expression` and
00731|   a :t:`break expression` broke out the :t:`block expression`, then the
00732|   :t:`value` is the :t:`break value` of the :t:`break expression` that
00733|   broke out of the :t:`block expression`.
00734| 
00735| * :dp:`fls_9nmssjseq3jt`
00736|   Otherwise, if the :t:`block expression` has a :t:`tail expression`, then the
00737|   :t:`value` is the :t:`value` of the :t:`tail expression`.
00738| 
00739| * :dp:`fls_a3ulnvyc1ut`
00740|   Otherwise the :t:`value` is the :t:`unit value`.
00741| 
00742| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00743| 
00744| :dp:`fls_elcl73psruxw`
00745| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`block expression` proceeds as follows:
00746| 
00747| #. :dp:`fls_13b5n127rj92`
00748|    Each :t:`statement` is executed in declarative order.
00749| 
00750| #. :dp:`fls_nzdpw59plr2g`
00751|    The :t:`tail expression` is evaluated.
00752| 
00753| .. rubric:: Examples
00754| 
00755| .. code-block:: rust
00756| 
00757|    {
00758|        fn_call();
00759|        42
00760|    }
00761| 
00762| .. _fls_aadan19t5006:
00763| 
00764| Async Blocks
00765| ~~~~~~~~~~~~
00766| 
00767| .. rubric:: Syntax
00768| 
00769| .. syntax::
00770| 
00771|    AsyncBlockExpression ::=
00772|        $$async$$ $$move$$? BlockExpression
00773| 
00774| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00775| 
00776| :dp:`fls_hhidi5ukxo`
00777| An :dt:`async block expression` is a :t:`block expression` that is specified
00778| with :t:`keyword` ``async`` and encapsulates behavior which is executed in
00779| an asynchronous manner.
00780| 
00781| :dp:`fls_DnCb6ei84CeI`
00782| An :dt:`async block` is an :t:`async block expression`.
00783| 
00784| :dp:`fls_h36F1drHlHkH`
00785| A :dt:`control flow boundary` is a :t:`construct` that provides the target of
00786| a :t:`return expression` and bounds its control flow transfer.
00787| 
00788| :dp:`fls_sRfEgIkgKhlk`
00789| An :dt:`async control flow boundary` is a :t:`control flow boundary` introduced
00790| by an :t:`async block expression`, :t:`async function`, or
00791| :t:`async closure expression`.
00792| 
00793| :dp:`fls_oisws5qykedi`
00794| An :t:`async block expression` denotes a new :t:`async control flow boundary`.
00795| 
00796| :dp:`fls_tzclkasinpoq`
00797| An :t:`async block expression` is subject to :t:`capturing`.
00798| 
00799| :dp:`fls_ncd0wkgtldem`
00800| The :t:`type` of an :t:`async block expression` is a unique anonymous :t:`type`
00801| that implement the :std:`core::future::Future` trait.
00802| 
00803| :dp:`fls_pvnofoomgwl5`
00804| The :t:`value` of an :t:`async block expression` is a :t:`value` of the
00805| :t:`async block expression`'s :t:`type`.
00806| 
00807| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00808| 
00809| :dp:`fls_9ghp5yet75y6`
00810| The :t:`evaluation` of an :t:`async block expression` produces a :t:`value` of
00811| the :t:`type` of the :t:`async block expression` that :t:`captures <capturing>`
00812| the :t:`[capture target]s` of the :t:`async block expression`.
00813| 
00814| .. rubric:: Examples
00815| 
00816| .. code-block:: rust
00817| 
00818|    async {
00819|        42
00820|    }
00821| 
00822| .. _fls_G59PiNQkVUnQ:

00824| Const Blocks
00825| ~~~~~~~~~~~~
00826| 
00827| .. rubric:: Syntax
00828| 
00829| .. syntax::
00830| 
00831|    ConstBlockExpression ::=

00834| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00835| 
00836| :dp:`fls_0lcunL4bo8ka`
00837| A :dt:`const block expression` is a :t:`block expression` that is specified
00838| with :t:`keyword` ``const`` and encapsulates behavior which is evaluated
00839| statically.
00840| 
00841| :dp:`fls_veEGzEbpT4ny`
00842| An :t:`const block expression` denotes a new :t:`control flow boundary`.
00843| 
00844| :dp:`fls_PiUS1hF3dv9U`
00845| The :t:`block expression` of a :t:`const block expression` shall be a

00847| 
00848| :dp:`fls_wuwb0SnpP6Zu`
00849| The :t:`type` of a :t:`const block expression` is the :t:`type` of the

00852| :dp:`fls_2i7TD7VoQk4B`

00855| 
00856| .. rubric:: Examples

00900| Unsafe Blocks
00901| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~

00903| .. rubric:: Syntax

00905| .. syntax::

00912| :dp:`fls_p1v7k4m2t9qa`

00915| :dp:`fls_2az5huhcxzzy`
00916| An :dt:`unsafe block expression` is a :t:`block expression` that is specified

00920| An :t:`unsafe block expression` allows :t:`unsafety`.

00946| Operator Expressions
00947| --------------------
00948| 
00949| .. rubric:: Syntax
00950| 
00951| .. syntax::
00952| 
00953|    OperatorExpression ::=
00954|        ArithmeticExpression
00955|      | AssignmentExpression
00956|      | BitExpression
00957|      | BorrowExpression
00958|      | ComparisonExpression
00959|      | CompoundAssignmentExpression
00960|      | DereferenceExpression
00961|      | ErrorPropagationExpression
00962|      | LazyBooleanExpression
00963|      | NegationExpression
00964|      | RawBorrowExpression
00965|      | TypeCastExpression
00966| 
00967| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00968| 
00969| :dp:`fls_ursc5ynymoy`
00970| An :dt:`operator expression` is an :t:`expression` that involves an operator.
00971| 
00972| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00973| 
00974| :dp:`fls_lSxXWxJn0vMO`
00975| An :t:`operator expression` that operates with :t:`[floating-point value]s` run as a :t:`constant expression` is allowed to yield different :t:`[value]s` compared to when run as a non-:t:`constant expression`.
00976| 
00977| .. _fls_qztk0bkju9u:
00978| 
00979| Borrow Expression
00980| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
00981| 
00982| .. rubric:: Syntax
00983| 
00984| .. syntax::
00985| 
00986|    BorrowExpression ::=
00987|        $$&$$ $$mut$$? Operand
00988| 
00989| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00990| 
00991| :dp:`fls_nnqfkl228hjx`
00992| A :dt:`borrow expression` is an :t:`expression` that borrows the :t:`value` of
00993| its :t:`operand` and creates a :t:`reference` to the memory location of its
00994| :t:`operand`.
00995| 
00996| :dp:`fls_r7ix8webgqlm`
00997| An :dt:`immutable borrow expression` is a :t:`borrow expression` that lacks
00998| :t:`keyword` ``mut``.
00999| 
01000| :dp:`fls_50j167r4v61b`
01001| A :dt:`mutable borrow expression` is a :t:`borrow expression` that has
01002| :t:`keyword` ``mut``.
01003| 
01004| :dp:`fls_ya77l2zgtilp`
01005| When the :t:`operand` of a :t:`borrow expression` is a :t:`place expression`,
01006| the :t:`borrow expression` produces a :t:`reference` to the memory location
01007| indicated by the :t:`operand`. The memory location is placed in a borrowed
01008| state, or simply :t:`borrowed`.
01009| 
01010| :dp:`fls_chr03xll75d`
01011| The :t:`type` of a :t:`borrow expression` is determined as follows:
01012| 
01013| * :dp:`fls_5b2x5ri2w54r`
01014|   If the :t:`borrow expression` denotes an :t:`immutable borrow expression`, then the
01015|   :t:`type` is ``&T``, where ``T`` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand`.
01016| 
01017| * :dp:`fls_agl09ia869rk`
01018|   If the :t:`borrow expression` denotes a :t:`mutable borrow expression`, then the
01019|   :t:`type` is ``&mut T``, where ``T`` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand`.
01020| 
01021| :dp:`fls_8cvmee9bzs40`
01022| The :t:`value` of a :t:`borrow expression` is the address of its :t:`operand`.
01023| 
01024| :dp:`fls_LuaPBicDlDTT`
01025| It is a static error if a :t:`borrow expression` would create an unaligned
01026| reference to a :t:`field` in an :t:`abstract data type` subject to
01027| :t:`attribute` :c:`repr`.
01028| 
01029| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
01030| 
01031| :dp:`fls_2jd0mgw4zja4`
01032| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`borrow expression` evaluates its :t:`operand`.
01033| 
01034| .. rubric:: Examples
01035| 
01036| .. code-block:: rust
01037| 
01038|    let mut answer = 42;
01039| 
01040| :dp:`fls_350qejoq9i23`
01041| Mutable borrow.
01042| 
01043| .. syntax::
01044| 
01045|    let ref_answer = &mut answer;
01046| 
01047| .. _fls_vXGuvRWOLbEE:
01048| 
01049| Raw Borrow Expression
01050| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01051| 
01052| .. rubric:: Syntax
01053| 
01054| .. syntax::
01055| 
01056|    RawBorrowExpression ::=
01057|        $$&$$ $$raw$$ ($$const$$ | $$mut$$) Operand
01058| 
01059| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01060| 
01061| :dp:`fls_TS6DvMon5h27`
01062| A :dt:`raw borrow expression` is an :t:`expression` that creates a :t:`raw pointer` to the memory location of its :t:`operand` without incurring a :t:`borrow`.
01063| 
01064| :dp:`fls_UtjWrE2qeplQ`
01065| An :dt:`immutable raw borrow expression` is a :t:`raw borrow expression` that has :t:`keyword` ``const``.
01066| 
01067| :dp:`fls_4e7EE4a8Yvmy`
01068| A :dt:`mutable raw borrow expression` is a :t:`raw borrow expression` that has :t:`keyword` ``mut``.
01069| 
01070| :dp:`fls_gOXUWePymgGV`
01071| When the :t:`operand` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` is a :t:`place expression`, the :t:`raw borrow expression` produces a :t:`raw pointer` to the memory location indicated by the :t:`operand`.
01072| 
01073| :dp:`fls_YBC8GrIBzZbi`
01074| It is a static error if the :t:`operand` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` is a :t:`temporary`.
01075| 
01076| :dp:`fls_Twkre8IzUa8S`
01077| The :t:`type` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` is determined as follows:
01078| 
01079| * :dp:`fls_Ki4FOzJMqtvJ`
01080|   If the :t:`raw borrow expression` denotes an :t:`immutable raw borrow expression`, then the :t:`type` is ``*const T``, where ``T`` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand`.
01081| 
01082| * :dp:`fls_DJxQDBsO9hc7`
01083|   If the :t:`raw borrow expression` denotes a :t:`mutable raw borrow expression`, then the :t:`type` is ``*mut T``, where ``T`` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand`.
01084| 
01085| :dp:`fls_WlXB0AHifCdd`
01086| The :t:`value` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` is the address of its :t:`operand`.
01087| 
01088| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
01089| 
01090| :dp:`fls_qQrV8QuGGcVO`
01091| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`raw borrow expression` evaluates its :t:`operand`.
01092| 
01093| .. rubric:: Examples
01094| 
01095| .. code-block:: rust
01096| 
01097|    let mut answer = 42;
01098| 
01099| :dp:`fls_dTABiwAPGhdZ`
01100| Mutable raw borrow.
01101| 
01102| .. syntax::
01103| 
01104|    let ref_answer = &raw mut answer;
01105| 
01106| .. _fls_5cm4gkt55hjh:
01107| 
01108| Dereference Expression
01109| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01110| 
01111| .. rubric:: Syntax
01112| 
01113| .. syntax::
01114| 
01115|    DereferenceExpression ::=
01116|        $$*$$ Operand
01117| 
01118| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01119| 
01120| :dp:`fls_f6wktzofzdn1`
01121| A :dt:`dereference expression` is an :t:`expression` that obtains the pointed-to
01122| memory location of its :t:`operand`.
01123| 
01124| :dp:`fls_aOXtV5sEUGxJ`
01125| A :dt:`dereference` is the memory location produced by evaluating a
01126| :t:`dereference expression`.
01127| 
01128| :dp:`fls_aeh5pzpcjveq`
01129| When the :t:`operand` of a :t:`dereference expression` is of a :t:`pointer
01130| type`, the :t:`dereference expression` denotes the pointed-to memory location of
01131| the :t:`operand`, or the :t:`dereference` of the :t:`operand`.
01132| 
01133| :dp:`fls_9cc0ml2sru6x`
01134| The :t:`dereference` is assignable when the :t:`dereference expression` is a
01135| :t:`mutable place expression`.
01136| 
01137| :dp:`fls_8i4jzksxlrw0`
01138| Dereferencing a :t:`raw pointer` shall require :t:`unsafe context` unless the :t:`dereference expression` is the :t:`operand` of a :t:`raw borrow expression`.
01139| 
01140| :dp:`fls_d68ddlse4zp`
01141| If the context of a :t:`dereference expression` is an
01142| :t:`immutable place expression context`, then the :t:`dereference expression`
01143| is equivalent to :t:`expression` ``*core::ops::Deref::deref(&operand)``.
01144| 
01145| :dp:`fls_g73vguanbs1x`
01146| If the context of a :t:`dereference expression` is a
01147| :t:`mutable place expression context`, then the :t:`dereference expression`
01148| is equivalent to :t:`expression`
01149| ``*core::ops::DerefMut::deref_mut(&mut operand)``.
01150| 
01151| :dp:`fls_8ibfqxtnahzx`
01152| The :t:`type` of a :t:`dereference expression` is determined as follows:
01153| 
01154| * :dp:`fls_7e7tka4f2f1a`
01155|   If the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand` is ``&mut T``, ``&T``, ``*mut T``, or
01156|   ``*const T``, then the :t:`type` is ``T``.
01157| 
01158| * :dp:`fls_y9bc691kkh6v`
01159|   Otherwise the :t:`type` is :t:`associated type`
01160|   :std:`core::ops::Deref::Target`.
01161| 
01162| :dp:`fls_gw49nukfveib`
01163| The :t:`value` of a :t:`dereference expression` is determined as follows:
01164| 
01165| * :dp:`fls_jjf3sz9ddfhy`
01166|   If the :t:`type` of the :t:`operand` is ``&mut T``, ``&T``, ``*mut T``, or
01167|   ``*const T``, then the :t:`value` is the pointed-to :t:`value`.
01168| 
01169| * :dp:`fls_fyend8kkpqq4`
01170|   Otherwise the :t:`value` is the result of evaluating :t:`expression`
01171|   ``*core::ops::Deref::deref(&operand)`` or :t:`expression`
01172|   ``*core::ops::DerefMut::deref_mut(&mut operand)`` respectively.
01173| 
01174| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
01175| 
01176| :dp:`fls_72bpdsxxbgeq`
01177| The :t:`evaluation` of a :t:`dereference expression` evaluates its :t:`operand`.
01178| 
01179| .. rubric:: Undefined Behavior
01180| 
01181| :dp:`fls_9wgldua1u8yt`
01182| It is undefined behavior to dereference a :t:`raw pointer` that is either
01183| :t:`dangling` or unaligned.
01184| 
01185| .. rubric:: Examples
01186| 
01187| :dp:`fls_9ifaterm8nop`
01188| See :p:`fls_350qejoq9i23` for the declaration of ``ref_answer``.
01189| 
01190| .. code-block:: rust
01191| 
01192|    let deref_answer = *ref_answer;
01193| 
01194| .. _fls_pocsh1neugpc:
01195| 
01196| Error Propagation Expression
01197| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01198| 
01199| .. rubric:: Syntax
01200| 
01201| .. syntax::
01202| 
01203|    ErrorPropagationExpression ::=
01204|        Operand $$?$$
01205| 
01206| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
01207| 
01208| :dp:`fls_8q59wbumrt5s`
01209| An :dt:`error propagation expression` is an :t:`expression` that either evaluates
01210| to a :t:`value` of its :t:`operand` or returns a value to the enclosing control
01211| flow boundary.
01212| 
01213| :dp:`fls_mq2h4seoxah`
01214| An :t:`error propagation expression` shall appear within a :t:`control flow
01215| boundary`.
01216| 
01217| :dp:`fls_ab4vhq4nwn7f`
01218| The :t:`type` of an :t:`error propagation expression` is :t:`associated type`
01219| :std:`core::ops::Try::Output`.
01220| 
01221| :dp:`fls_z4zikxy2b1em`
01222| The :t:`value` of an :t:`error propagation expression` is determined as follows:
01223| 
01224| * :dp:`fls_a09614kgsspt`
01225|   If the :t:`evaluation` of the :t:`error propagation expression` executed
01226|   :std:`core::ops::Try::branch`, then the :t:`value` is the :t:`value` of
01227|   the :std:`core::ops::ControlFlow::Continue` variant.
01228| 
01229| * :dp:`fls_8df018q7y6g`
01230|   Otherwise control flow is returned to the end of the enclosing :t:`control
01231|   flow boundary`.
01232| 
01233| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
01234| 
01235| :dp:`fls_alk4qvfprnvy`
01236| The :t:`evaluation` of an :t:`error propagation expression` of the form
01237| 
01238| .. code-block:: rust
01239| 
01240|    expression?
01241| 
01242| :dp:`fls_1nnhjcgy8kdh`
01243| is equivalent to the :t:`evaluation` the following :t:`expression`:
01244| 
01245| .. code-block:: rust
01246| 
01247|    match core::ops::Try::branch(expression) {
01248|        core::ops::ControlFlow::Continue(value) =>
01249|            value,
01250| 
01251|        core::ops::ControlFlow::Break(value) =>
01252|            core::ops::FromResidual::from_residual(value),
01253|    }
01254| 
01255| .. rubric:: Examples
01256| 
01257| .. code-block:: rust
01258| 
01259|    fn try_to_parse() -> Result<i32, ParseIntError> {
01260|        "42".parse()?
01261|    }
01262| 
01263|    fn try_some() -> Option<i32> {
01264|        let val = Some(42)?;
01265|        Some(val)

:t:`borrow`.

01691| address that is a multiple of ``N``.
01692| 
01693| :dp:`fls_1pbwigq6f3ha`
01694| The :dt:`size` of a :t:`type` is the offset in bytes between successive elements
01695| in :t:`array type` ``[T, N]`` where ``T`` is the :t:`type` of the :t:`value`,
01696| including any padding for :t:`alignment`. :t:`Size` is a multiple of the
01697| :t:`alignment`.
01698| 
01699| :dp:`fls_B2pGRVJlOihB`
01700| A :dt:`layout` specifies the :t:`alignment`, :t:`size`, and the relative offset
01701| of :t:`[field]s` in a :t:`type`.
01702| 
01703| :dp:`fls_bk3nm2n47afu`
01704| The :t:`size` of :t:`[scalar type]s` is as follows:
01705| 
01706| .. list-table::
01707| 
01708|    * - :dp:`fls_z3i758jshvhx`
01709|      - **Type**
01710|      - **Size**
01711|    * - :dp:`fls_uixe1ruv52be`
01712|      - :c:`bool`
01713|      - 1
01714|    * - :dp:`fls_7at60xlxm9u4`
01715|      - :c:`u8`, :c:`i8`
01716|      - 1
01717|    * - :dp:`fls_395247pkxv48`
01718|      - :c:`u16`, :c:`i16`

02460| Structural Equality
02461| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
02462| 
02463| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
02464|

02467| for equality by structure.

02494| Interior Mutability
02495| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
02496| 
02497| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
02498| 
02499| :dp:`fls_khy2e23i9o7z`
02500| :dt:`Interior mutability` is a property of :t:`[type]s` whose :t:`[value]s` can

02507| .. _fls_mcxF9y5u66sZ:
02508| 
02509| Visible Emptiness
02510| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
02511| 
02512| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
02513| 
02514| :dp:`fls_SD4yUEQ9hHa3`
02515| :dt:`Visible emptiness <visible emptiness>` is a property of :t:`[type]s` and :t:`[enum variant]s` that have no :t:`[value]s` that are fully observable.
02516| 
02517| :dp:`fls_GeoneCP5TYwf`
02518| A :dt:`visible empty type` is a :t:`type` subject to :t:`visible emptiness`.
02519| 
02520| :dp:`fls_A2W4v53ihTGx`
02521| A :dt:`visible empty enum variant` is an :t:`enum variant` subject to :t:`visible emptiness`.
02522| 
02523| :dp:`fls_AXOtKdSQR4AF`
02524| A :t:`type` is subject to :t:`visible emptiness` as follows:
02525| 
02526| * :dp:`fls_ZfnYORORz40y`
02527|   The :t:`type` is the :t:`never type`.
02528| 
02529| * :dp:`fls_TE2GBnZX2YFW`
02530|   The :t:`type` is a :t:`zero-variant enum type`.
02531| 
02532| * :dp:`fls_GNFzLMH8RBUC`
02533|   The :t:`type` is an :t:`enum type` not subject to :t:`attribute` :c:`non_exhaustive` where all :t:`[enum variant]s` are subject to :t:`visible emptiness`.
02534| 
02535| * :dp:`fls_fSNrRsgzLd0E`
02536|   The :t:`type` is a :t:`struct type` with at least one :t:`visible <visibility>` :t:`field` whose :t:`type` is subject to :t:`visible emptiness`.
02537| 
02538| * :dp:`fls_R65LmGTvSlIm`
02539|   The :t:`type` is a :t:`tuple type` with at least one of the contained :t:`[type]s` is subject to :t:`visible emptiness`.
02540| 
02541| * :dp:`fls_yiPv3wKYkSRl`
02542|   The :t:`type` is an :t:`array type` with a non-zero :t:`size operand` and an :t:`element type` that is subject to :t:`visible emptiness`.
02543| 
02544| :dp:`fls_S9QL6yVF5LFI`
02545| A :t:`enum variant` is subject to :t:`visible emptiness` when the :t:`type` of at least one of the :t:`enum variant`'s :t:`visible <visibility>` :t:`[field]s` is subject to :t:`visible emptiness`.
02546| 
02547| .. _fls_lv7w7aalpwm5:
02548| 
02549| Type Inference
02550| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
02551| 
02552| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
02553| 
02554| :dp:`fls_h8sedxew0d4u`
02555| :dt:`Type inference` is the process of automatically determining the :t:`type` of
02556| :t:`[expression]s` and :t:`[pattern]s` within a :t:`type inference root`.
02557| 
02558| :dp:`fls_ybvrhh96fc7y`
02559| A :dt:`type inference root` is an :t:`expression` whose inner :t:`[expression]s`
02560| and :t:`[pattern]s` are subject to :t:`type inference` independently of those
02561| found in other :t:`[type inference root]s`.
02562| 
02563| :dp:`fls_EWBilpepaDcX`
02564| The following :t:`[expression]s` are considered :t:`[type inference root]s`:
02565| 
02566| * :dp:`fls_NYSzcvf5nQpi`
02567|   A :t:`constant argument`.
02568| 
02569| * :dp:`fls_htLp5J5ObgNh`
02570|   The :t:`expression` of a :t:`constant initializer`.
02571| 
02572| * :dp:`fls_cPlCLGCcl7EK`
02573|   The :t:`expression` of a :t:`static initializer`.
02574| 
02575| * :dp:`fls_KphY5qHev0Dc`
02576|   The :t:`expression` of a :t:`discriminant initializer`.
02577| 
02578| * :dp:`fls_67Bf5kR5OtYW`
02579|   The :t:`expression` of a :t:`constant parameter initializer`.
02580| 
02581| * :dp:`fls_sAS3vQpkjksr`
02582|   The :t:`expression` of a :t:`constant argument`.
02583| 
02584| * :dp:`fls_Sowatt1V988J`
02585|   A :t:`function body`.
02586| 
02587| * :dp:`fls_A1NVYkepoaMk`
02588|   The :t:`size operand` of an :t:`array expression` or an :t:`array type`.
02589| 
02590| :dp:`fls_J77VeAlD8rsv`
02591| A :t:`type inference root` imposes an :dt:`expected type` on its :t:`expression`
02592| depending on the :t:`type inference root` as follows:
02593| 
02594| * :dp:`fls_P9uiamxA4HE3`
02595|   The :t:`expected type` of a :t:`constant argument` is the :t:`type ascription`
02596|   of the :t:`constant parameter`.
02597| 
02598| * :dp:`fls_5d4hw3gj4w4n`
02599|   The :t:`expected type` of the :t:`expression` of a :t:`constant initializer`
02600|   is the :t:`type` specified by the :t:`type ascription` of the related
02601|   :t:`constant`.
02602| 
02603| * :dp:`fls_qlovdtcj1v1b`
02604|   The :t:`expected type` of the :t:`expression` of a :t:`static initializer` is
02605|   the :t:`type` specified by the :t:`type ascription` of the related
02606|   :t:`static`.
02607| 
02608| * :dp:`fls_Z5gKFjZW5rRA`
02609|   The :t:`expected type` of the :t:`expression` of a
02610|   :t:`discriminant initializer` is determined as follows:
02611| 
02612|   * :dp:`fls_vYvumjTQH9Xg`
02613|     If the :t:`enum type` that contains the :t:`discriminant` is subject to
02614|     :t:`attribute` :c:`repr` that specifies a :t:`primitive representation`, the
02615|     :t:`expected type` is the specified :t:`integer type`.
02616| 
02617|   * :dp:`fls_QaGKt99CmvF6`
02618|     Otherwise, the :t:`expected type` is :c:`isize`.
02619| 
02620| * :dp:`fls_RJIeW597XRvS`
02621|   The :t:`expected type` of a :t:`function body` is the :t:`return type` of the
02622|   :t:`function`.
02623| 
02624| * :dp:`fls_veG2D64fIXvo`
02625|   The :t:`expected type` of a :t:`size operand` of an :t:`array expression` or
02626|   an :t:`array type` is :c:`usize`.
02627| 
02628| :dp:`fls_uvvn4usfsbhr`
02629| A :dt:`type variable` is a placeholder used during :t:`type inference` to stand
02630| in for an undetermined :t:`type` of an :t:`expression` or a :t:`pattern`.
02631| 
02632| :dp:`fls_gDalJm1XS0mi`
02633| A :dt:`global type variable` is a :t:`type variable` that can refer to any
02634| :t:`type`.
02635| 
02636| :dp:`fls_7ov36fpd9mwe`
02637| An :dt:`integer type variable` is a :t:`type variable` that can refer only to
02638| :t:`[integer type]s`.
02639| 
02640| :dp:`fls_3hv3wxkhjjp1`
02641| A :dt:`floating-point type variable` is a :t:`type variable` that can refer only
02642| to :t:`[floating-point type]s`.
02643| 
02644| :dp:`fls_bXQ63GYYDuMp`
02645| A :dt:`diverging type variable` is a :t:`type variable` that can refer to any
02646| :t:`type` and originates from a :t:`diverging expression`.
02647| 
02648| :dp:`fls_JryXiKBIFvF3`
02649| A :dt:`lifetime variable` is a placeholder used during :t:`type inference` to
02650| stand in for an undetermined :t:`lifetime` of a :t:`type`.
02651| 
02652| :dp:`fls_rvj3XspFZ1u3`
02653| The :t:`type inference` algorithm uses :t:`type unification` to propagate known
02654| :t:`[type]s` of :t:`[expression]s` and :t:`[pattern]s` across the
02655| :t:`type inference root` being inferred. In the rules detailed below, a static
02656| error occurs when :t:`type unification` fails.
02657| 
02658| :dp:`fls_6GrNr2izovRN`
02659| Performing :t:`type inference` may introduce a requirement that some :t:`type`
02660| must implement a :t:`trait`, or that a :t:`type` or :t:`lifetime` must outlive
02661| some other :t:`lifetime`. Such requirements are referred to as
02662| :dt:`[obligation]s` and are detailed in the inference rules below.
02663| 
02664| :dp:`fls_9dSltJ6U98Fo`
02665| If insufficient :t:`type` information is available at the time an
02666| :t:`obligation` is introduced, it may be deferred to be resolved later.
02667| Any time new :t:`type` information is derived during :t:`type inference`, the
02668| tool attempts to resolve all outstanding :t:`[obligation]s` and propagate
02669| any resulting :t:`type` information via :t:`type unification`.
02670| 
02671| :dp:`fls_v5dWGuBKvQSJ`
02672| When an :t:`associated type` ``<Type as Trait>::Assoc`` is referenced within a
02673| :t:`type inference root` (either explicitly within the source code, or via the
02674| inference rules below), an :t:`obligation` requiring that ``Type`` implements
02675| ``Trait`` is introduced.
02676| 
02677| :dp:`fls_SZgixDCAx6PQ`
02678| :t:`Type inference` for a :t:`type inference root` proceeds as follows:
02679| 
02680| #. :dp:`fls_XYY1U9h9HlAa`
02681|    Recursively process all :t:`[expression]s` and :t:`[statement]s` in the
02682|    :t:`type inference root` in program order.
02683| 
02684|    #. :dp:`fls_1rnssw39aRWn`
02685|       For each :t:`statement`, apply the :t:`statement` inference rules outlined below.
02686| 
02687|    #. :dp:`fls_aYJaZXcOVVyk`
02688|       For each :t:`expression`, apply the :t:`expression` inference rules outlined below.
02689| 
02690| #. :dp:`fls_X8kLC7JwiF0A`
02691|    If there are any remaining :t:`[integer type variable]s` that have not been
02692|    unified with a concrete :t:`integer type`, perform integer type fallback by
02693|    unifying them with :c:`i32`.
02694| 
02695| #. :dp:`fls_ZFQhOxO3jpby`
02696|    If there are any remaining :t:`[floating-point type variable]s` that have not
02697|    been unified with a concrete :t:`floating-point type`, perform floating-point
02698|    type fallback by unifying them with :c:`f64`.
02699| 
02700| #. :dp:`fls_l1G52a0qqEes`
02701|    If there are any remaining :t:`[diverging type variable]s` that have not been
02702|    unified with a concrete :t:`type`, unify them with the :t:`unit type`.
02703|
